2606.11264.title: OmniBioTwin: A System-of-Twinned-Systems Framework for Health Digital Twins
2606.11264.paper_summary: Health digital twins (HDTs) promise patient-specific modeling and decision support but current approaches remain structurally fragmented: monolithic models that address a single organ or task lack cross-scale fidelity, while system-level twins lack generalizable architectural frameworks. We propose OmniBioTwin, a System-of-Twinned-Systems (SoTS) framework that organizes HDTs as modular computational entities coupled through explicit interaction operators within a multi-layer network architecture. The framework comprises seven coordinated layers - spanning data integration, autonomous twin modeling, cross-scale coupling, temporal synchronization, and human-in-the-loop decision support. We demonstrate OmniBioTwin by instantiating a multiscale twin for glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) signaling pathways in Alzheimer's disease, illustrating how molecular, cellular, and organ-level twins can be composed and coupled within a unified system.
2606.11264.paper_summary_zh: 健康數位雙胞胎（HDTs）承諾提供病人特定的建模和決策支持，但目前的方法仍然結構性分散：針對單一器官或任務的單體模型缺乏跨尺度的真實性，而系統級雙胞胎則缺乏可普遍應用的架構框架。<lb/>我們提出OmniBioTwin，一個雙重系統的系統（SoTS）框架，將HDTs組織為通過明確的互動運算子耦合的模組化計算實體，並置於多層網絡架構中。<lb/>該框架包括七個協調層級——涵蓋數據整合、自主雙胞胎建模、跨尺度耦合、時間同步及人類參與的決策支持。<lb/>我們通過實例化阿茲海默症中胰高血糖素樣肽-1（GLP-1）信號通路的多尺度雙胞胎來展示OmniBioTwin，說明如何在統一系統中組合和耦合分子、細胞及器官層級的雙胞胎。
2606.11264.author: Zhaohui Wang et.al.
2606.11264.authors: Zhaohui Wang, Yu Huang, Jiang Bian
